sections.title: NL-Clicker Plugin API SDK
sections.text: Reference documentation for developing plugins for the NL-Clicker application.

sections.title: REGISTRY ARCHITECTURE
sections.text: The system utilizes two global repositories (Singletons) to facilitate communication between the plugin and the core.

blocks.title: 1. UIRegistry (Anchor Registry)
blocks.content:
Purpose: Stores references to the physical frames (ctk.CTkFrame) of the main application.
Access: Plugins access these via string aliases (e.g., "sidebar", "footer", "MainWindowPluginFrame") defined by the core.
Permissions: Public get / try_get methods are open to all plugins.

blocks.title: 2. RedactableDataRegistry (Data Registry)
blocks.content: Purpose: A universal store for class objects, logic modules, settings, or primitive variables (int, str, bool).

rows: get / try_get methods are restricted. They can only be invoked through PluginContext. Accessing them from the Global Scope will trigger a PermissionError.
bind / bind_by_type methods are open, allowing plugins to share their own objects or data with others.

blocks.title: Usage
blocks.content: from Registers import ui_registry as uireg, redactable_data_registry as rdr

sections.title: UI INTERFACE
sections.text: Methods for mounting and managing user interface elements.

rows:
Mounts a widget into a predefined core frame.
- anchor_name (str): Target frame alias from UIRegistry.
- widget_cls (class): The widget class to instantiate (e.g., ctk.CTkButton).
- weight (int): Sorting order. 0 for top, 100 for bottom, -1 to append.
- **kwargs: Args passed directly to the widget (text, command, etc).
Adds a widget to the plugin's private dedicated window.
Replaces the entire content of the plugin window with a new container (e.g., ctk.CTkScrollableFrame). This clears the window before insertion.
Removes a specific widget instance from the UI.
Instantly destroys all UI elements created by the plugin.
Initializes the window. Use fix_size='500x500' to disable resizing.
show_window(), hide_window(), lift_window(), center_window() - Standard controls.
Sets a function to be executed when the window's close (X) button is pressed.

sections.title: DATA & RESOURCES
sections.text: Handling external files and remote data.

rows: Updates an attribute of an object or a primitive in RedactableDataRegistry. Type must match.
Returns bytes of a file located inside the .nlp package (e.g., 'assets/icon.png').
Store a dictionary in the application's AppData folder.
Retrieve a dictionary from the application's AppData folder.

blocks.title: Logging & Core Access
blocks.content:
log(message, level="INFO"): Sends a message to the core logger. Levels: "INFO", "WARN", "ERROR".
main_window (Property): A direct reference to the main application window. Use this as the master for your own custom Toplevel windows or complex widgets.

sections.title: QUICK START GUIDE
sections.text: Step-by-step guide to build and deploy your plugin.

blocks.title: 1. Directory Structure
blocks.content:
MyPlugin/
├── manifest.json      # Metadata (required)
├── main.py            # Entry point (required)
├── assets/            # Resources (optional)
└── libs/              # External .pyd/.py libraries (optional)

blocks.title: 3. Entry Point (main.py)
4. Deployment
blocks.content:
1. Select the contents of MyPlugin/.
2. Create a ZIP archive.
3. Rename the extension to .nlp.
4. Place it in %APPDATA%/NL-clicker/plugins/.
